seoTitle: Longsys Storage Products | eMMC, SSDs, Memory Modules - LiTong
seoDescription: Browse Longsys embedded storage, SSDs, and memory modules. Technical specifications and selection guide from authorized distributor.

faqs.question: What product categories does Longsys offer?
faqs.answer: Longsys offers four primary product categories: Embedded Storage including eMMC, UFS, and eMCP for mobile and automotive applications; Solid State Drives including SATA and NVMe SSDs for consumer, industrial, and enterprise use; Memory Modules including DDR4 and DDR5 for desktop, laptop, and server applications; and Portable Storage including USB drives, portable SSDs, and memory cards. Each category includes multiple product families optimized for specific performance, capacity, and reliability requirements. As an authorized distributor, LiTong provides comprehensive selection guidance across all product lines.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our product categories below to find detailed specifications and selection guides. Contact our FAE team for assistance in selecting the optimal storage solutions for your application.

faqs.question: How do I select the right Longsys embedded storage for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right Longsys embedded storage requires understanding your application requirements. For smartphones and tablets, UFS provides the highest performance with sequential read speeds up to 2100MB/s. For cost-sensitive applications, eMMC 5.1 offers excellent value with read speeds up to 400MB/s. For automotive applications, select AEC-Q100 qualified products with wide temperature range (-40°C to +105°C). Consider capacity requirements, interface compatibility, endurance (TBW), and operating temperature range. Longsys provides detailed datasheets and application notes to guide your selection process.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review the detailed specifications in our Embedded Storage category. Contact LiTong FAEs for personalized recommendations based on your specific requirements.

faqs.question: What is the difference between consumer-grade and industrial-grade SSDs?
faqs.answer: Consumer-grade SSDs are designed for typical consumer applications with standard operating temperature ranges (0°C to 70°C) and moderate endurance ratings. Industrial-grade SSDs feature wider operating temperature ranges (-40°C to +85°C), higher endurance (higher TBW ratings), power loss protection, and enhanced data retention. Industrial SSDs also undergo more stringent testing for reliability in harsh environments. Longsys offers both grades to match specific application requirements and cost targets.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select consumer-grade for standard applications, industrial-grade for harsh environments and mission-critical systems.

faqs.question: What form factors are available for Longsys SSDs?
faqs.answer: Longsys SSDs are available in multiple form factors to meet different application requirements. 2.5-inch SATA SSDs are ideal for laptop upgrades and industrial applications. M.2 2280 SSDs support both SATA and NVMe interfaces for compact systems. M.2 2242 and 2230 are available for ultra-compact devices. mSATA SSDs serve legacy industrial systems. U.2 and E1.S form factors are available for enterprise and data center applications. All form factors support various capacities from 128GB to 8TB depending on the product family.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select form factor based on your system interface and physical space constraints.

faqs.question: What DDR memory speeds does Longsys offer?
faqs.answer: Longsys offers DDR4 and DDR5 memory modules with various speed grades. DDR4 modules are available from 2133MT/s to 3200MT/s, suitable for current generation platforms. DDR5 modules range from 4800MT/s to 5600MT/s, providing higher bandwidth for next-generation systems. All modules are available in UDIMM, SODIMM, and RDIMM form factors for desktop, laptop, and server applications respectively. Longsys memory modules undergo rigorous testing to ensure compatibility and reliability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select DDR4 for current platforms, DDR5 for next-generation systems requiring higher bandwidth.

categories.name: Embedded Storage
categories.description: Longsys embedded storage solutions provide high-performance, reliable NAND flash storage for mobile, automotive, and industrial applications.
categories.longDescription: Longsys embedded storage solutions deliver high-performance, reliable NAND flash storage for a wide range of applications. The comprehensive portfolio includes eMMC 5.1 and UFS 2.1/3.1 for mobile devices, automotive-grade eMMC and UFS with AEC-Q100 qualification, and eMCP for space-constrained applications. These products feature advanced NAND flash management, error correction, and wear leveling algorithms to ensure data integrity and long service life. With capacities ranging from 4GB to 512GB and operating temperature ranges from consumer to industrial grades, Longsys embedded storage serves smartphones, tablets, automotive infotainment systems, industrial control, and IoT applications. As an authorized Longsys distributor, LiTong provides comprehensive selection guidance, technical support, and reliable supply chain services for these essential storage components.

selectionGuide.title: Embedded Storage Selection Guide
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting embedded storage based on performance, capacity, and reliability requirements

faqs.question: What is the difference between eMMC and UFS?
faqs.answer: eMMC (embedded MultiMediaCard) and UFS (Universal Flash Storage) are both embedded storage solutions but with different performance characteristics. eMMC 5.1 uses a parallel interface with sequential read speeds up to 400MB/s, suitable for cost-sensitive applications. UFS uses a serial M-PHY interface with full-duplex communication, providing sequential read speeds up to 2100MB/s for UFS 3.1. UFS offers significantly better performance for high-end smartphones and applications requiring fast data access. eMMC remains popular for mid-range devices and industrial applications due to its cost-effectiveness and mature ecosystem.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select UFS for high-performance applications, eMMC for cost-sensitive designs.

faqs.question: What capacities are available for Longsys embedded storage?
faqs.answer: Longsys embedded storage is available in a wide range of capacities to meet different application requirements. eMMC products range from 4GB to 256GB, suitable for IoT devices to tablets. UFS products range from 32GB to 512GB, targeting high-end smartphones and automotive applications. eMCP products combine NAND flash with LPDDR memory, available from 8GB+1GB to 128GB+8GB configurations. Automotive-grade products follow the same capacity ranges with enhanced reliability features for harsh environments.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select capacity based on application storage requirements with 20-30% headroom for future expansion.

faqs.question: What is the endurance rating of Longsys embedded storage?
faqs.answer: Longsys embedded storage endurance is rated in TBW (Terabytes Written) or program/erase cycles. Consumer-grade eMMC typically supports 3,000 to 5,000 P/E cycles, while industrial-grade supports 10,000+ P/E cycles. UFS products offer similar endurance ratings with higher performance. Automotive-grade products are optimized for the specific write patterns of automotive applications with enhanced endurance. The actual lifespan depends on the write amplification factor and application usage patterns. Longsys provides endurance calculation tools to estimate product life based on specific use cases.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select industrial-grade for high-write applications, consumer-grade for typical usage.

faqs.question: What temperature ranges are supported?
faqs.answer: Longsys embedded storage supports various temperature ranges to meet different application requirements. Consumer-grade products operate from 0°C to 70°C, suitable for typical consumer electronics. Industrial-grade products support -40°C to +85°C for harsh industrial environments. Automotive-grade products support -40°C to +105°C (Grade 2) or -40°C to +85°C (Grade 3) per AEC-Q100 standards. Wide temperature range products undergo additional testing and screening to ensure reliability at temperature extremes.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select temperature grade based on operating environment requirements.

faqs.question: What is the difference between TLC and pSLC mode?
faqs.answer: Longsys embedded storage uses TLC (Triple-Level Cell) NAND as the base technology, offering high capacity at competitive cost. pSLC (pseudo-SLC) mode configures TLC NAND to store 1 bit per cell instead of 3 bits, significantly improving endurance (10x improvement) and performance while reducing capacity to 1/3. pSLC mode is ideal for applications requiring high reliability and endurance such as industrial control and automotive systems. Longsys offers products with both standard TLC and pSLC configurations to match specific application requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select pSLC mode for high-endurance applications, TLC for standard capacity requirements.

products.name: 64GB Automotive eMMC 5.1
products.shortDescription: AEC-Q100 Grade 2 automotive eMMC 5.1 with 64GB capacity, -40°C to +105°C range for automotive applications
descriptionParagraphs: The FEMDNN064G-A3A55 is an AEC-Q100 Grade 2 qualified automotive eMMC 5.1 storage solution designed for demanding automotive applications.
With 64GB capacity, HS400 interface mode, and wide temperature range operation, it is ideal for automotive infotainment and ADAS systems.
The device includes advanced error correction, power-loss protection, and enhanced endurance for automotive reliability requirements.

faeReview.author: Dr. Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Principal FAE - Automotive Storage
faeReview.content: The FEMDNN064G-A3A55 is my go-to recommendation for automotive storage applications requiring AEC-Q100 qualification. The 64GB capacity is well-suited for modern infotainment systems with navigation maps and media storage. I have successfully used this eMMC in multiple automotive projects with excellent reliability. The -40°C to +105°C temperature range handles under-hood and cabin environments. The HS400 interface provides adequate performance for boot and application loading. The power-loss protection is essential for automotive applications where unexpected power events can occur. For automotive storage requiring AEC-Q100 compliance, this product offers excellent value and reliability.
faeReview.highlight: AEC-Q100 Grade 2 automotive eMMC with excellent reliability for infotainment applications

alternativeParts.reason: Micron offers similar automotive specs but at higher price point
Kioxia offers industrial grade but narrower temperature range

companionParts.description: UFS upgrade for higher performance needs

faqs.question: What is the maximum interface speed of this eMMC?
faqs.answer: The FEMDNN064G-A3A55 supports eMMC 5.1 HS400 mode with maximum interface speed of 400MB/s. Actual sequential read performance is up to 320MB/s and sequential write up to 90MB/s. The HS400 mode uses 8-bit parallel interface with DDR (Double Data Rate) operation at 200MHz clock frequency. This provides excellent performance for automotive boot and application loading requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: HS400 mode provides excellent performance for automotive infotainment boot and media storage.

faqs.question: Does this eMMC support power-loss protection?
faqs.answer: Yes, the FEMDNN064G-A3A55 includes power-loss protection features to prevent data corruption during unexpected power events. The firmware implements sudden power-off recovery mechanisms to maintain data integrity. This is critical for automotive applications where power events can occur during engine cranking or electrical transients. The power-loss protection ensures filesystem consistency and prevents bricking of the storage device.
faqs.decisionGuide: Power-loss protection is essential for automotive applications with potential power instability.

faqs.question: What is the endurance rating for automotive applications?
faqs.answer: The FEMDNN064G-A3A55 is rated for 3,000 to 5,000 P/E (Program/Erase) cycles under typical automotive usage patterns. With Longsys advanced wear leveling algorithms, this translates to several years of service life in typical infotainment applications. For applications with higher write requirements, consider pSLC mode products or larger capacity devices which distribute writes across more NAND cells.
faqs.decisionGuide: Endurance is sufficient for typical automotive infotainment read-heavy workloads.

faqs.question: What package options are available?
faqs.answer: The FEMDNN064G-A3A55 is available in standard BGA-153 package with 11.5mm x 13mm x 1.0mm dimensions. The BGA-153 package is the industry standard for eMMC devices and is compatible with most automotive-grade PCB assembly processes. The package uses lead-free (RoHS compliant) solder balls with standard 0.5mm ball pitch for reliable surface mount assembly.
faqs.decisionGuide: BGA-153 package is compatible with standard automotive PCB assembly processes.

faqs.question: What AEC-Q100 grade is this product qualified to?
faqs.answer: The FEMDNN064G-A3A55 is qualified to AEC-Q100 Grade 2, which requires operation from -40°C to +105°C ambient temperature. This grade is suitable for automotive interior applications and some under-hood applications with appropriate thermal management. Grade 2 qualification includes rigorous testing for temperature cycling, high-temperature operating life, ESD protection, and mechanical shock.
faqs.decisionGuide: AEC-Q100 Grade 2 is suitable for automotive interior and controlled under-hood applications.

products.name: 128GB UFS 3.1
products.shortDescription: High-performance UFS 3.1 with 128GB capacity, sequential read up to 2100MB/s for flagship mobile devices
descriptionParagraphs: The FEMDNN128G-58A43 is a high-performance UFS 3.1 storage solution designed for flagship smartphones and performance-critical applications.
With 128GB capacity, sequential read speeds up to 2100MB/s, and MIPI M-PHY 4.1 interface, it delivers exceptional storage performance.
The device supports advanced features including WriteBooster, DeepSleep power mode, and hardware-based encryption.

faeReview.author: Jennifer Liu
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Mobile Storage
faeReview.content: The FEMDNN128G-58A43 is an excellent UFS 3.1 solution for high-performance mobile applications. The 2100MB/s sequential read speed enables fast app launches and smooth 4K video recording. I have used this UFS in multiple flagship smartphone projects with outstanding performance. The WriteBooster feature significantly improves burst write performance for camera applications. The DeepSleep mode helps extend battery life in mobile devices. The 128GB capacity is the sweet spot for current flagship devices. For premium mobile devices requiring top-tier storage performance, this UFS 3.1 product delivers excellent results at competitive pricing compared to international brands.
faeReview.highlight: High-performance UFS 3.1 with 2100MB/s read speed for flagship mobile devices

alternativeParts.reason: Samsung offers similar performance but at premium pricing
Kioxia offers similar UFS 3.1 with slightly lower performance

companionParts.description: LPDDR5 memory for complete mobile solution

faqs.question: What is the maximum speed of UFS 3.1 compared to UFS 2.1?
faqs.answer: UFS 3.1 provides significant performance improvements over UFS 2.1. Sequential read speeds increased from ~900MB/s (UFS 2.1) to ~2100MB/s (UFS 3.1), and sequential write from ~250MB/s to ~1200MB/s. The M-PHY 4.1 interface in UFS 3.1 supports 11.6Gbps per lane compared to 5.8Gbps in UFS 2.1. Additionally, UFS 3.1 introduces WriteBooster for enhanced sustained write performance and DeepSleep for improved power efficiency.
faqs.decisionGuide: UFS 3.1 provides 2x performance improvement over UFS 2.1 for demanding applications.

faqs.question: What is WriteBooster and how does it improve performance?
faqs.answer: WriteBooster is a UFS 3.1 feature that uses SLC caching to accelerate write performance. When enabled, a portion of the TLC NAND operates in SLC mode to absorb burst writes at higher speeds. This significantly improves sustained write performance for applications like 4K video recording and burst photography. The WriteBooster cache dynamically manages data migration from SLC to TLC, optimizing both performance and endurance.
faqs.decisionGuide: WriteBooster is essential for applications requiring high sustained write performance.

faqs.question: Does UFS 3.1 support hardware encryption?
faqs.answer: Yes, UFS 3.1 supports hardware-based encryption through the UFS standard's security features. The device supports RPMB (Replay Protected Memory Block) for secure storage of critical data. Additionally, UFS supports inline encryption engines that can perform AES encryption/decryption without host processor involvement, improving both security and performance. These features are essential for protecting user data on mobile devices.
faqs.decisionGuide: Hardware encryption provides data security without performance penalty.

faqs.question: What is the power consumption of UFS 3.1?
faqs.answer: UFS 3.1 power consumption varies by operating mode. Active sequential read consumes approximately 1.5-2.5W, while sequential write consumes 2-3W. The DeepSleep mode reduces idle power consumption to less than 1mW, significantly extending battery life in mobile devices. UFS 3.1 also supports various power modes (Active, Sleep, Power-down) that can be controlled by the host to optimize power consumption based on usage patterns.
faqs.decisionGuide: DeepSleep mode provides excellent power efficiency for battery-powered devices.

faqs.question: Is UFS backward compatible with older host controllers?
faqs.answer: UFS devices are backward compatible with older UFS host controllers, but will operate at the lower interface speed. For example, a UFS 3.1 device connected to a UFS 2.1 host will operate at UFS 2.1 speeds (~900MB/s read). The device negotiates the maximum supported speed during the link startup sequence. For optimal performance, both host and device should support UFS 3.1 with M-PHY 4.1 interface.
faqs.decisionGuide: For full UFS 3.1 performance, ensure host controller supports UFS 3.1.

products.name: 256GB Automotive eMMC 5.1

The device includes advanced error correction, power-loss protection, enhanced endurance for automotive reliability, and supports extended temperature operation from -40°C to +105°C.

faeReview.content: The FEMDNN256G-A3A55 is the ideal solution for automotive applications requiring large storage capacity. The 256GB capacity accommodates modern infotainment systems with high-resolution maps, media libraries, and application storage. I have successfully deployed this eMMC in premium vehicle infotainment systems with excellent reliability. The AEC-Q100 Grade 2 qualification ensures reliable operation in automotive environments. The HS400 interface provides adequate performance for boot and application loading. The enhanced endurance rating supports the long service life required in automotive applications. For automotive storage requiring high capacity and AEC-Q100 compliance, this product delivers excellent performance and reliability.
faeReview.highlight: 256GB automotive eMMC with AEC-Q100 Grade 2 qualification for high-capacity automotive storage

alternativeParts.reason: Micron offers similar automotive specs but at higher price point

faqs.question: What is the endurance rating of the 256GB automotive eMMC?
faqs.answer: The FEMDNN256G-A3A55 is rated for 3,000 to 5,000 P/E (Program/Erase) cycles. With 256GB capacity and advanced wear leveling, this provides excellent endurance for automotive applications. The large capacity distributes writes across more NAND cells, effectively increasing endurance compared to lower capacity devices.
faqs.decisionGuide: 256GB capacity provides excellent endurance for automotive applications through wear distribution.

products.name: 64GB UFS 2.1
products.shortDescription: UFS 2.1 storage with 64GB capacity, sequential read up to 900MB/s for mid-range mobile devices
descriptionParagraphs: The FEMDNN064G-58A43 is a UFS 2.1 storage solution designed for mid-range smartphones and mobile devices requiring better performance than eMMC.
With 64GB capacity, sequential read speeds up to 900MB/s, and MIPI M-PHY 3.0 interface, it delivers significantly better performance than eMMC 5.1 alternatives.
The device supports advanced features including WriteBooster, DeepSleep power mode, and is available in standard BGA-153 package for easy integration.

faeReview.author: Jennifer Liu
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Mobile Storage
faeReview.content: The FEMDNN064G-58A43 is an excellent choice for mid-range mobile devices requiring better performance than eMMC. The UFS 2.1 interface provides 2-3x better performance than eMMC 5.1, enabling faster app launches and smoother multitasking. The 64GB capacity is suitable for mid-range devices with moderate storage requirements. I have used this UFS in several mid-range smartphone projects with good results. The WriteBooster feature helps with burst write performance for camera applications. The DeepSleep mode helps extend battery life. For mid-range devices where UFS 3.1 is not cost-effective, this UFS 2.1 product provides excellent performance at a more competitive price point.
faeReview.highlight: UFS 2.1 with 900MB/s read speed for mid-range mobile devices requiring better than eMMC performance

alternativeParts.reason: Samsung offers similar UFS 2.1 performance

faqs.question: How does UFS 2.1 compare to eMMC 5.1 performance?
faqs.answer: UFS 2.1 provides significantly better performance than eMMC 5.1. Sequential read speeds are approximately 900MB/s vs 320MB/s (2.8x faster), and sequential write speeds are approximately 250MB/s vs 90MB/s (2.8x faster). Random IOPS performance is also much better, with UFS 2.1 providing up to 45K read IOPS vs ~8K for eMMC 5.1.
faqs.decisionGuide: UFS 2.1 provides 2-3x better performance than eMMC 5.1 for demanding applications.

categories.name: Solid State Drives
categories.description: Longsys SSDs provide high-performance, reliable storage for consumer, industrial, and enterprise applications with SATA and NVMe interfaces.
categories.longDescription: Longsys solid-state drives deliver high-performance, reliable storage solutions for diverse applications. The comprehensive portfolio includes SATA III SSDs for mainstream applications and NVMe PCIe SSDs for high-performance requirements. Available in multiple form factors including 2.5-inch, M.2, and mSATA, these SSDs serve consumer upgrades, industrial systems, and enterprise servers. With capacities ranging from 128GB to 8TB and endurance ratings from consumer to industrial grades, Longsys SSDs offer solutions for every application. Advanced features include power-loss protection, hardware encryption, and thermal throttling for reliable operation. As an authorized Longsys distributor, LiTong provides comprehensive selection guidance, technical support, and reliable supply chain services for these essential storage components.

selectionGuide.title: SSD Selection Guide
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting SSDs based on performance, capacity, and application requirements

faqs.question: What is the difference between SATA and NVMe SSDs?
faqs.answer: SATA SSDs use the SATA III interface with maximum theoretical speed of 6Gbps (约550MB/s). NVMe SSDs use PCIe interface with speeds up to 7GB/s for PCIe 4.0 x4. NVMe offers significantly lower latency and higher IOPS, making it ideal for high-performance applications. SATA remains popular for cost-sensitive applications and legacy system upgrades. Longsys offers both interfaces to match specific performance and budget requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select NVMe for high-performance applications, SATA for cost-sensitive or legacy upgrades.

faqs.question: What is TBW and why does it matter?
faqs.answer: TBW (Terabytes Written) indicates the total amount of data that can be written to an SSD before wear-out. Consumer SSDs typically offer 150-600 TBW, while industrial SSDs offer 1000+ TBW. Higher TBW ratings indicate longer lifespan for write-intensive applications. TBW is calculated based on NAND P/E cycles and over-provisioning. For typical consumer use, even 150 TBW provides several years of service life.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select higher TBW for write-intensive applications like video editing or database servers.

faqs.question: What form factor should I choose for my application?
faqs.answer: 2.5-inch SATA is ideal for laptop upgrades and desktop systems. M.2 2280 is the most common form factor for modern systems supporting both SATA and NVMe. M.2 2242/2230 are for compact devices. mSATA is for legacy industrial systems. U.2 is for enterprise servers. Choose based on your system's physical space and interface support.
faqs.decisionGuide: Check your system's available slots and interface support before selecting form factor.

faqs.question: Do Longsys SSDs support hardware encryption?
faqs.answer: Yes, Longsys SSDs support AES-256 hardware encryption through TCG Opal 2.0 and IEEE 1667 standards. Hardware encryption provides full-disk security without performance penalty. The encryption engine operates at full interface speed, ensuring no slowdown during encrypted operations. This feature is essential for protecting sensitive data on laptops and enterprise systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: Enable hardware encryption for systems containing sensitive data.

faqs.question: What is power-loss protection in SSDs?
faqs.answer: Power-loss protection uses onboard capacitors to provide emergency power during unexpected shutdowns. This allows the SSD to complete in-flight writes and flush the DRAM cache to NAND, preventing data corruption. Industrial and enterprise SSDs include this feature as standard. Consumer SSDs may offer basic protection through firmware algorithms without dedicated capacitors.
faqs.decisionGuide: Power-loss protection is essential for industrial and enterprise applications.

products.name: 1TB SATA III 2.5-inch SSD
products.shortDescription: High-capacity 1TB SATA III SSD with 2.5-inch form factor, sequential read up to 560MB/s
descriptionParagraphs: The FS01TB-35M41 is a high-capacity SATA III SSD delivering reliable performance for consumer and industrial applications.
With 1TB capacity, 560MB/s sequential read speed, and 2.5-inch form factor, it is ideal for laptop upgrades and industrial systems.
The drive includes advanced error correction, wear leveling, and optional power-loss protection for data integrity.

faeReview.author: David Wang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Storage Solutions
faeReview.content: The FS01TB-35M41 is an excellent SATA SSD for mainstream applications. The 1TB capacity meets most consumer and light industrial needs. I have deployed this SSD in numerous laptop upgrade projects with excellent user feedback. The 560MB/s read speed saturates the SATA III interface, providing maximum performance for this interface. The SLC cache handles burst writes effectively for typical consumer workloads. The 7mm slim design fits modern laptops and ultrabooks. The optional industrial temperature grade (-40°C to +85°C) makes it suitable for industrial applications. For cost-effective SATA storage, this product offers excellent value.
faeReview.highlight: High-capacity SATA SSD with excellent price-performance for consumer and industrial use

alternativeParts.reason: Crucial MX500 offers similar specs with established brand recognition
Samsung 870 EVO offers similar performance at premium price

companionParts.description: NVMe upgrade for higher performance needs
DDR4 memory for complete system upgrade

faqs.question: What is the actual usable capacity of the 1TB SSD?
faqs.answer: The FS01TB-35M41 has a formatted capacity of approximately 931GB. The difference between manufacturer TB (1TB = 1,000,000,000,000 bytes) and computer TiB (1TiB = 1,099,511,627,776 bytes) accounts for this difference. Additionally, a small portion of NAND is reserved for over-provisioning to improve endurance and performance. This is standard across all SSDs and is not specific to Longsys products.
faqs.decisionGuide: 931GB formatted capacity is standard for 1TB SSDs across all manufacturers.

faqs.question: Does this SSD support TRIM command?
faqs.answer: Yes, the FS01TB-35M41 fully supports the TRIM command for maintaining optimal performance over time. TRIM allows the operating system to inform the SSD which data blocks are no longer in use, enabling the SSD to proactively erase them. This prevents performance degradation that can occur with sustained write operations. TRIM is supported on Windows 7 and later, modern Linux distributions, and macOS.
faqs.decisionGuide: TRIM is enabled by default on modern operating systems and requires no user action.

faqs.question: What is the warranty period for this SSD?
faqs.answer: The FS01TB-35M41 comes with a 3-year limited warranty or 600 TBW (Terabytes Written), whichever comes first. The warranty covers defects in materials and workmanship under normal use. Industrial-grade versions may have extended warranty options. The TBW rating indicates the total amount of data that can be written to the drive while maintaining warranty coverage.
faqs.decisionGuide: 3-year warranty with 600 TBW provides excellent coverage for typical consumer use.

faqs.question: Can this SSD be used in RAID configurations?
faqs.answer: Yes, the FS01TB-35M41 supports RAID configurations when used with appropriate RAID controllers. The drive is compatible with hardware RAID controllers and software RAID implementations in Windows, Linux, and other operating systems. For RAID setups, it is recommended to use identical drives with the same capacity and firmware version for optimal performance and compatibility.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use identical drives for RAID configurations to ensure optimal performance.

faqs.question: What is the power consumption of this SSD?
faqs.answer: The FS01TB-35M41 has typical active power consumption of 2.5W during read operations and 3.5W during write operations. Idle power consumption is approximately 0.5W, and DEVSLP (Device Sleep) mode consumes less than 5mW. These low power characteristics make the drive suitable for laptops and battery-powered systems where power efficiency is important.
faqs.decisionGuide: Low power consumption makes this SSD ideal for laptops and energy-efficient systems.

products.name: 1TB NVMe M.2 2280 SSD
products.shortDescription: High-performance 1TB NVMe PCIe 3.0 x4 SSD with M.2 2280 form factor, sequential read up to 3500MB/s
descriptionParagraphs: The FN01TB-80A22 is a high-performance NVMe SSD delivering exceptional speed for demanding applications.
With 1TB capacity, PCIe 3.0 x4 interface, and 3500MB/s sequential read speed, it is ideal for gaming and professional workstations.
The drive features advanced thermal management, hardware encryption, and high endurance for reliable operation.

faeReview.author: Jennifer Liu
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - High-Performance Storage
faeReview.content: The FN01TB-80A22 delivers excellent NVMe performance at competitive pricing. The 3500MB/s read speed provides noticeable improvement over SATA SSDs for boot times and application loading. I have used this SSD in gaming builds and workstation upgrades with excellent results. The thermal management prevents throttling under sustained workloads. The hardware encryption is valuable for professional users handling sensitive data. The 600 TBW endurance rating is appropriate for typical consumer and professional use. For users seeking high-performance storage without premium pricing, this NVMe SSD is an excellent choice.
faeReview.highlight: High-performance NVMe SSD with 3500MB/s read speed for gaming and professional use

alternativeParts.reason: Samsung offers slightly higher write speed at premium price
alternativeParts.useCase: Applications requiring Samsung brand and maximum performance

alternativeParts.reason: WD Black offers similar performance with gaming-focused branding

companionParts.description: High-capacity DDR4 for complete system upgrade
Thermal solution for sustained NVMe performance

faqs.question: What is the difference between PCIe 3.0 and PCIe 4.0 NVMe SSDs?
faqs.answer: PCIe 4.0 NVMe SSDs offer double the bandwidth of PCIe 3.0, with sequential read speeds up to 7000MB/s compared to 3500MB/s for PCIe 3.0. However, PCIe 4.0 requires a compatible motherboard and CPU. For most consumer applications, the difference is not noticeable in daily use. PCIe 3.0 SSDs like the FN01TB-80A22 offer excellent performance at more affordable pricing and broader compatibility.
faqs.decisionGuide: PCIe 3.0 SSDs offer excellent performance and value for most applications.

faqs.question: Does this SSD require a heatsink?
faqs.answer: The FN01TB-80A22 includes basic thermal management and can operate without a heatsink in most systems with adequate airflow. However, for sustained heavy workloads or systems with limited airflow, adding an M.2 heatsink can help maintain peak performance by preventing thermal throttling. The drive includes thermal throttling protection that reduces performance if temperatures exceed safe limits.
faqs.decisionGuide: Heatsink recommended for sustained heavy workloads or compact systems.

faqs.question: What is the difference between 3D TLC and QLC NAND?
faqs.answer: The FN01TB-80A22 uses 3D TLC (Triple-Level Cell) NAND which stores 3 bits per cell, offering better endurance and performance than QLC (Quad-Level Cell) which stores 4 bits per cell. TLC typically offers 3,000-5,000 P/E cycles compared to 1,000 or less for QLC. While QLC offers lower cost per GB, TLC provides better sustained performance and longevity for primary storage applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: TLC NAND offers better balance of performance, endurance, and cost for primary storage.

faqs.question: Can this SSD be used in a PS5?
faqs.answer: The FN01TB-80A22 uses PCIe 3.0 interface and does not meet PS5 requirements which specify PCIe 4.0 SSDs with minimum 5500MB/s sequential read speed. For PS5 expansion, Longsys offers PCIe 4.0 NVMe SSDs that meet Sony's specifications. Please check our PCIe 4.0 product lineup for PS5-compatible options.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select PCIe 4.0 NVMe SSDs for PS5 compatibility.

faqs.question: What is the MTBF rating for this SSD?
faqs.answer: The FN01TB-80A22 has an MTBF (Mean Time Between Failures) rating of 1.5 million hours, indicating high reliability for consumer and professional use. This rating is based on accelerated life testing and statistical analysis. Combined with the 600 TBW endurance rating and 3-year warranty, this SSD provides reliable storage for typical consumer and professional workloads.
faqs.decisionGuide: 1.5M hour MTBF provides excellent reliability for consumer and professional use.

products.name: 2TB SATA III 2.5-inch SSD
products.shortDescription: High-capacity 2TB SATA III SSD with 2.5-inch form factor, sequential read up to 560MB/s for high-capacity storage
descriptionParagraphs: The FS02TB-35M41 is a high-capacity SATA III SSD delivering reliable performance and large storage capacity for demanding applications.
With 2TB capacity, 560MB/s sequential read speed, and 2.5-inch form factor, it is ideal for high-capacity storage upgrades and media servers.
The drive includes advanced error correction, wear leveling, and optional power-loss protection for data integrity in high-capacity applications.

faeReview.author: David Zhang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Storage Solutions
faeReview.content: The FS02TB-35M41 is an excellent high-capacity SATA SSD for applications requiring large storage. The 2TB capacity accommodates extensive media libraries, game collections, and large datasets. I have deployed this SSD in media servers and gaming systems with excellent results. The performance is consistent with SATA III limits, providing 560MB/s read speeds. The 1,200 TBW endurance rating supports heavy write workloads. The 7mm form factor fits in laptops and compact systems. For high-capacity SATA storage where cost per GB is important, this 2TB SSD offers excellent value and reliability.
faeReview.highlight: 2TB SATA SSD with excellent capacity and reliability for high-capacity storage applications

alternativeParts.reason: Samsung offers similar performance but at premium pricing

companionParts.description: NVMe SSD for higher performance needs

faqs.question: What is the endurance rating of the 2TB SSD?
faqs.answer: The FS02TB-35M41 has an endurance rating of 1,200 TBW (Terabytes Written). This high endurance rating supports heavy write workloads and provides long service life. For typical consumer use, this translates to many years of reliable operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: 1,200 TBW provides excellent endurance for high-capacity storage applications.

products.name: 2TB NVMe M.2 2280 SSD
products.shortDescription: High-performance 2TB NVMe PCIe 3.0 x4 SSD with M.2 2280 form factor, sequential read up to 3500MB/s
descriptionParagraphs: The FN02TB-80A22 is a high-performance NVMe SSD with 2TB capacity for demanding applications requiring large high-speed storage.
With PCIe 3.0 x4 interface, 3500MB/s sequential read speed, and M.2 2280 form factor, it delivers exceptional performance for gaming and professional workstations.
The drive features advanced thermal management, hardware encryption, high endurance, and 2TB capacity for large game libraries and professional datasets.

faeReview.author: David Zhang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Storage Solutions
faeReview.content: The FN02TB-80A22 is an excellent high-capacity NVMe SSD for demanding applications. The 2TB capacity accommodates large game libraries, video projects, and professional datasets. The 3500MB/s read speed delivers exceptional performance for gaming and content creation. I have deployed this SSD in high-end gaming systems and video editing workstations with outstanding results. The thermal management prevents throttling during sustained workloads. The hardware encryption provides security without performance penalty. For high-capacity NVMe storage requiring top-tier performance, this 2TB SSD delivers excellent results.
faeReview.highlight: 2TB NVMe SSD with 3500MB/s speed for high-performance gaming and professional applications

alternativeParts.reason: Samsung PCIe 4.0 offers higher performance but at much higher cost

faqs.question: What is the difference between the 1TB and 2TB NVMe SSDs?
faqs.answer: The FN02TB-80A22 offers the same high performance as the 1TB version (3500MB/s read, 3000MB/s write) but with double the capacity. The 2TB version has higher endurance (1,200 TBW vs 600 TBW) and is ideal for users requiring large high-speed storage for game libraries, video projects, or professional datasets.
faqs.decisionGuide: 2TB provides same performance with double capacity and higher endurance.

categories.name: Memory Modules
categories.description: Longsys DDR4 and DDR5 memory modules provide high-speed, reliable memory for desktop, laptop, and server applications.
categories.longDescription: Longsys memory modules deliver high-speed, reliable DDR4 and DDR5 solutions for diverse computing applications. The product portfolio includes UDIMM for desktop systems, SODIMM for laptops and compact systems, and RDIMM for servers. With speeds ranging from DDR4-2133 to DDR5-5600, Longsys memory supports current and next-generation platforms. All modules undergo rigorous testing including 100% burn-in testing to ensure compatibility and reliability. As an authorized Longsys distributor, LiTong provides comprehensive selection guidance, technical support, and reliable supply chain services for these essential memory components.

selectionGuide.title: Memory Module Selection Guide
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting DDR4 and DDR5 memory based on platform and performance requirements

faqs.question: What is the difference between DDR4 and DDR5?
faqs.answer: DDR5 offers significant improvements over DDR4 including higher bandwidth (starting at 4800MT/s vs 2133MT/s), improved power efficiency (1.1V vs 1.2V), and on-die ECC for better reliability. DDR5 modules include PMIC (Power Management IC) on the module for better voltage regulation. However, DDR5 requires compatible motherboards and CPUs. DDR4 remains excellent for current platforms with mature ecosystem and competitive pricing.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select DDR5 for new next-generation platforms, DDR4 for current systems.

faqs.question: What do the memory speed numbers mean?
faqs.answer: Memory speed is rated in MT/s (MegaTransfers per second). DDR4-3200 operates at 3200MT/s with actual clock frequency of 1600MHz (DDR transfers data on both clock edges). Higher numbers indicate faster memory bandwidth. However, actual performance depends on CPU memory controller and application memory access patterns. For most applications, DDR4-3200 provides excellent performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: DDR4-3200 offers excellent price-performance for most applications.

faqs.question: What is the difference between UDIMM and SODIMM?
faqs.answer: UDIMM (Unbuffered DIMM) is the standard memory module for desktop PCs with 288 pins (DDR4) or 288 pins (DDR5). SODIMM (Small Outline DIMM) is a compact version with 260 pins (DDR4) or 262 pins (DDR5) designed for laptops and compact systems. The modules are not interchangeable due to different physical sizes and pin configurations. Always check your system's memory slot type before purchasing.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select UDIMM for desktops, SODIMM for laptops and compact systems.

faqs.question: Can I mix different memory speeds?
faqs.answer: While possible, mixing different memory speeds is not recommended. When mixed, all modules will run at the speed of the slowest module. For best performance and stability, use identical modules with the same capacity, speed, and timings. If mixing is necessary, ensure all modules support the lowest common speed and timings.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use identical memory modules for best performance and compatibility.

faqs.question: What is XMP and do I need it?
faqs.answer: XMP (Extreme Memory Profile) is Intel's technology for running memory above standard JEDEC speeds. AMD uses similar AMP or DOCP profiles. Enabling XMP in BIOS allows memory to run at its rated speed (e.g., DDR4-3600) rather than default (DDR4-2133/2400). For high-speed memory modules, XMP is essential to achieve advertised performance. Most gaming and performance motherboards support XMP.
faqs.decisionGuide: Enable XMP in BIOS to achieve rated memory speeds.

products.name: 16GB DDR4-3200 UDIMM
products.shortDescription: High-capacity 16GB DDR4-3200 desktop memory module with CL22 latency for mainstream and gaming systems
descriptionParagraphs: The LD4U320016G is a high-capacity DDR4-3200 memory module designed for desktop PCs and workstations.
With 16GB capacity, 3200MT/s speed, and low-profile design, it is ideal for gaming and content creation.
The module supports XMP 2.0 for easy overclocking and features high-quality components for reliable operation.

faeReview.author: David Wang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Memory Solutions
faeReview.content: The LD4U320016G is an excellent DDR4-3200 module for mainstream and gaming systems. The 16GB capacity is the sweet spot for current gaming and productivity applications. I have used these modules in numerous builds with excellent compatibility across Intel and AMD platforms. The XMP profile works reliably on most motherboards for easy setup. The standard height fits most CPU coolers without clearance issues. The lifetime warranty provides peace of mind for end users. For DDR4 systems requiring reliable, high-capacity memory, this module offers excellent value.
faeReview.highlight: High-capacity DDR4-3200 module with excellent compatibility for gaming and productivity

alternativeParts.reason: Corsair offers lower latency at premium price
G.Skill offers gaming-focused branding with lower latency
alternativeParts.useCase: Gaming builds preferring premium memory brands

faqs.question: Is this memory compatible with my motherboard?
faqs.answer: The LD4U320016G is compatible with DDR4 motherboards supporting 3200MT/s speed. It works with both Intel and AMD platforms. For Intel, 8th generation Core and newer support DDR4-3200. For AMD, Ryzen 2000 series and newer support this speed. The module will downclock to the maximum supported speed if your platform supports lower speeds. Check your motherboard QVL (Qualified Vendor List) for guaranteed compatibility.
faqs.decisionGuide: Check motherboard QVL for guaranteed compatibility with specific models.

faqs.question: What is the difference between single-rank and dual-rank memory?
faqs.answer: The LD4U320016G is a dual-rank module, meaning memory chips are arranged on both sides of the PCB. Dual-rank modules can offer slightly better performance in some applications due to rank interleaving. However, single-rank modules may overclock better and consume slightly less power. For most users, the difference is minimal and either type works well. Ensure your motherboard supports the rank configuration you choose.
faqs.decisionGuide: Dual-rank offers good performance and compatibility for most applications.

faqs.question: Can I use this module with existing memory?
faqs.answer: Mixing memory modules is possible but not recommended for optimal performance. If mixing, all modules will run at the speed and timings of the slowest module. For best results, use identical modules with the same capacity, speed, and timings. If you must mix, ensure all modules support the lowest common speed and test thoroughly for stability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use identical modules for best performance and stability.

faqs.question: Does this memory require XMP to run at 3200MT/s?
faqs.answer: Yes, the LD4U320016G requires XMP (Extreme Memory Profile) to be enabled in BIOS to run at 3200MT/s. Without XMP, it will run at JEDEC standard speed of 2133MT/s or 2400MT/s depending on platform. Enabling XMP is simple and safe - just select the XMP profile in BIOS. Most gaming and performance motherboards support XMP.
faqs.decisionGuide: Enable XMP in BIOS to achieve rated 3200MT/s speed.

faqs.question: What is the warranty for this memory module?
faqs.answer: The LD4U320016G comes with a limited lifetime warranty covering defects in materials and workmanship. The warranty is valid for the original purchaser and requires proof of purchase. Longsys memory modules undergo rigorous testing including 100% burn-in to ensure reliability. The lifetime warranty reflects confidence in product quality and provides peace of mind for end users.
faqs.decisionGuide: Lifetime warranty provides excellent coverage for memory products.

products.name: 16GB DDR5-4800 UDIMM
products.shortDescription: Next-generation 16GB DDR5-4800 desktop memory module with improved bandwidth and power efficiency for latest platforms
descriptionParagraphs: The LD5U480016G is a next-generation DDR5-4800 memory module delivering improved performance and efficiency.
With 16GB capacity, 4800MT/s speed, and on-die ECC, it is ideal for Intel 12th/13th Gen and AMD AM5 platforms.
The module features onboard PMIC for improved power management and supports XMP 3.0 for easy configuration.

faeReview.author: Jennifer Liu
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Next-Gen Memory
faeReview.content: The LD5U480016G delivers excellent DDR5 performance for next-generation platforms. The 4800MT/s speed provides significant bandwidth improvement over DDR4. I have used these modules in Intel 13th Gen builds with excellent stability and performance. The on-die ECC improves data integrity without system-level ECC overhead. The XMP 3.0 profile works reliably on Z790 motherboards. The 1.1V operating voltage improves power efficiency. For new builds on DDR5 platforms, this module offers excellent performance and value compared to premium brands.
faeReview.highlight: Next-generation DDR5-4800 with on-die ECC for latest platforms

alternativeParts.reason: Corsair offers higher speed at premium price
G.Skill offers higher speed and lower latency at premium price

faqs.question: Is DDR5 backward compatible with DDR4 motherboards?
faqs.answer: No, DDR5 is not backward compatible with DDR4. DDR5 uses a different pin configuration (288 pins but different notch position), different voltage (1.1V vs 1.2V), and requires DDR5-compatible memory controllers. You must use DDR5 with motherboards specifically designed for DDR5, such as Intel 600/700 series or AMD AM5 platforms. DDR4 and DDR5 modules cannot be mixed or interchanged.
faqs.decisionGuide: DDR5 requires DDR5-compatible motherboard and CPU - not backward compatible.

faqs.question: What is on-die ECC in DDR5?
faqs.answer: DDR5 introduces on-die ECC (Error Correction Code) which corrects bit errors within the memory chip itself before sending data to the CPU. This improves data integrity and reliability compared to DDR4. Note that this is different from system-level ECC used in servers. On-die ECC operates transparently and does not require special support from the CPU or motherboard. This feature benefits all DDR5 systems by reducing silent data corruption.
faqs.decisionGuide: On-die ECC improves reliability for all DDR5 systems without special configuration.

faqs.question: What is PMIC in DDR5 memory?
faqs.answer: DDR5 moves the Power Management IC (PMIC) from the motherboard to the memory module itself. This provides better voltage regulation and power delivery to the memory chips. The onboard PMIC enables more precise voltage control for improved stability and overclocking potential. It also reduces complexity on the motherboard. The PMIC operates automatically and requires no user configuration.
faqs.decisionGuide: Onboard PMIC improves power delivery and stability for DDR5 modules.

faqs.question: Should I upgrade to DDR5 now or wait?
faqs.answer: For new builds on latest platforms (Intel 12th/13th Gen, AMD AM5), DDR5 is the best choice as these platforms are optimized for DDR5. For existing DDR4 systems, upgrading to DDR5 requires new motherboard and CPU, making it a significant investment. DDR5 prices have decreased significantly and are now competitive with high-end DDR4. Performance advantage varies by application - most noticeable in memory-intensive tasks and integrated graphics.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose DDR5 for new latest-gen builds, keep DDR4 for existing systems.

faqs.question: What platforms support DDR5-4800?
faqs.answer: DDR5-4800 is supported by Intel 12th Gen (Alder Lake) and 13th Gen (Raptor Lake) Core processors on 600/700 series motherboards. AMD AM5 platform with Ryzen 7000 series also supports DDR5-4800 as baseline speed. Most DDR5 motherboards support even higher speeds through XMP/EXPO profiles. Check your specific motherboard QVL for guaranteed memory compatibility and maximum supported speed.
faqs.decisionGuide: DDR5-4800 is baseline speed for Intel 12th/13th Gen and AMD AM5 platforms.

categories.name: Portable Storage
categories.description: Longsys portable storage solutions including USB flash drives, portable SSDs, and memory cards for consumer and professional use.
categories.longDescription: Longsys portable storage provides convenient, high-speed data storage and transfer solutions for mobile users. The product portfolio includes USB 3.2 flash drives for everyday file transfer, high-speed portable SSDs for professionals requiring fast external storage, and memory cards (SD/microSD) for cameras, drones, and mobile devices. With capacities up to 2TB for portable SSDs and 1TB for memory cards, Longsys portable storage meets diverse capacity and performance requirements. As an authorized Longsys distributor, LiTong provides comprehensive selection guidance and technical support for these portable storage solutions.

selectionGuide.title: Portable Storage Selection Guide
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting USB drives, portable SSDs, and memory cards

faqs.question: What is the difference between USB 3.0, 3.1, and 3.2?
faqs.answer: USB 3.0 (now called USB 3.2 Gen 1) offers 5Gbps speed. USB 3.1 Gen 2 (now USB 3.2 Gen 2) offers 10Gbps. USB 3.2 Gen 2x2 offers 20Gbps. For flash drives, actual performance is often limited by NAND flash speed rather than interface. Most USB flash drives use USB 3.2 Gen 1 (5Gbps) which is sufficient for typical flash drive speeds up to 400MB/s.
faqs.decisionGuide: USB 3.2 Gen 1 (5Gbps) is sufficient for most flash drives.

faqs.question: What speed class should I choose for microSD cards?
faqs.answer: For general use (smartphones, tablets), Class 10 or UHS-I U1 is sufficient. For 4K video recording, choose UHS-I U3 or UHS-II U3 (minimum 30MB/s write). For action cameras and drones, V30 or higher is recommended. For professional video, consider V60 or V90 cards. Higher speed classes provide better performance but at higher cost.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose V30 or higher for 4K video, U3 for Full HD video, U1 for general use.

faqs.question: What is the difference between portable SSD and USB flash drive?
faqs.answer: Portable SSDs use SSD technology with higher performance (up to 1000MB/s+), higher capacities (up to 2TB+), and better endurance. USB flash drives use simpler controller technology with lower performance (up to 400MB/s), smaller capacities (up to 1TB), and lower cost. Portable SSDs are ideal for professional use, large file transfers, and running applications. Flash drives are suitable for everyday file storage and transfer.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose portable SSD for performance and capacity, flash drive for portability and cost.

faqs.question: Do I need a special card for 4K video recording?
faqs.answer: Yes, 4K video recording requires memory cards with sufficient write speed. Look for UHS Speed Class 3 (U3) or Video Speed Class 30 (V30) minimum, which guarantee 30MB/s sustained write speed. For high bitrate 4K or 8K video, consider V60 or V90 cards. Using insufficient cards can result in recording errors, dropped frames, or corruption. Always check your camera's recommended card specifications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use V30 or higher rated cards for reliable 4K video recording.

faqs.question: Are portable SSDs durable for travel?
faqs.answer: Longsys portable SSDs are designed for mobility with shock and vibration resistance. Unlike HDDs, SSDs have no moving parts, making them inherently more durable for travel. However, they should still be protected from extreme temperatures, moisture, and physical impact. Many portable SSDs include rubber bumpers or metal enclosures for additional protection. Always safely eject before disconnecting to prevent data corruption.
faqs.decisionGuide: Portable SSDs are excellent for travel - more durable than HDDs with no moving parts.

products.name: 1TB USB 3.2 Portable SSD
products.shortDescription: Compact 1TB portable SSD with USB 3.2 Gen 2 interface, sequential read up to 1050MB/s for professionals on the go
descriptionParagraphs: The FP01TB-U32 is a high-performance portable SSD delivering professional-grade speed in a compact form factor.
With 1TB capacity, USB 3.2 Gen 2 interface, and 1050MB/s read speed, it is ideal for content creators and professionals.
The drive features rugged aluminum construction, hardware encryption, and cross-platform compatibility.

faeReview.author: David Wang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Portable Storage
faeReview.content: The FP01TB-U32 is an excellent portable SSD for professionals requiring fast external storage. The 1050MB/s speed enables direct editing of 4K video from the drive without performance issues. I have used this drive for on-location photography shoots with excellent reliability. The compact size fits easily in camera bags. The aluminum construction provides good heat dissipation and durability. Cross-platform compatibility means it works seamlessly between Windows and Mac systems. For professionals needing fast, portable storage, this drive offers excellent performance at competitive pricing.
faeReview.highlight: High-speed portable SSD with 1050MB/s read for professional content creation

alternativeParts.reason: SanDisk Extreme offers similar specs with established brand
Samsung T7 offers similar performance at premium price

faqs.question: What cable comes with this portable SSD?
faqs.answer: The FP01TB-U32 includes a USB-C to USB-C cable and a USB-C to USB-A adapter for compatibility with both modern and legacy systems. The cable supports USB 3.2 Gen 2 speeds up to 10Gbps. For best performance, use the included cable or a high-quality USB 3.2 Gen 2 certified cable. Lower quality cables may limit performance to USB 3.0 speeds (5Gbps).
faqs.decisionGuide: Use included cable or certified USB 3.2 Gen 2 cable for maximum performance.

faqs.question: Is this drive compatible with gaming consoles?
faqs.answer: The FP01TB-U32 is compatible with PlayStation 4/5 and Xbox Series X/S for external game storage. For PS5, it can store PS4 games and transfer PS5 games, but cannot run PS5 games directly (requires internal SSD expansion). For Xbox, it can store and play Xbox One, 360, and original Xbox games, and store Series X/S games. The USB 3.2 Gen 2 interface provides fast game loading from external storage.
faqs.decisionGuide: Compatible with gaming consoles for external storage and backward compatible games.

faqs.question: Does this drive require external power?
faqs.answer: No, the FP01TB-U32 is bus-powered through the USB connection and requires no external power adapter. The drive draws power from the USB port (up to 7.5W for USB 3.2). This makes it truly portable and convenient for use with laptops and mobile devices. Ensure your USB port provides sufficient power - most USB 3.0+ ports supply adequate power.
faqs.decisionGuide: Bus-powered through USB - no external power required.

faqs.question: Can I use this drive for Time Machine backup on Mac?
faqs.answer: Yes, the FP01TB-U32 is compatible with macOS and can be used for Time Machine backups. The drive comes pre-formatted as exFAT for cross-platform compatibility. For Time Machine, you may want to reformat as APFS or HFS+ using Disk Utility. The 1TB capacity provides ample space for most Mac system backups. The USB-C connection works directly with modern MacBooks.
faqs.decisionGuide: Fully compatible with macOS and Time Machine backup.

faqs.question: What is the shock resistance rating?
faqs.answer: The FP01TB-U32 is rated for shock resistance up to 1500G/0.5ms, providing protection against drops and impacts during travel. The solid-state design with no moving parts makes it inherently more durable than hard drives. The aluminum enclosure provides additional structural protection. However, while shock-resistant, the drive should still be protected from extreme impacts and drops onto hard surfaces.
faqs.decisionGuide: 1500G shock resistance provides excellent protection for travel and field use.

products.name: 256GB microSD V30
products.shortDescription: High-speed 256GB microSD card with V30 rating, up to 100MB/s read speed for 4K video recording and mobile expansion
descriptionParagraphs: The FM256-V30 is a high-performance microSD card designed for 4K video recording and mobile device expansion.
With 256GB capacity, V30 video speed class, and 100MB/s read speed, it is ideal for action cameras, drones, and smartphones.
The card is A2 rated for app performance and includes SD adapter for versatility.

faeReview.author: Jennifer Liu
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Memory Cards
faeReview.content: The FM256-V30 is an excellent microSD card for 4K video recording and mobile expansion. The V30 rating ensures reliable 4K recording at high bitrates. I have tested this card in GoPro and DJI drones with consistent performance. The 256GB capacity provides hours of 4K recording. The A2 rating means apps can run smoothly when installed on the card. The included SD adapter adds versatility for use in cameras. For users needing reliable, high-capacity microSD storage, this card offers excellent value compared to premium brands.
faeReview.highlight: V30 rated microSD with 256GB capacity for reliable 4K video recording

alternativeParts.reason: SanDisk Extreme offers faster read speed at premium price
Samsung EVO offers faster read but lower write speed

companionParts.description: Portable SSD for large file backup
USB card reader for fast transfers

faqs.question: How much 4K video can 256GB store?
faqs.answer: The actual recording time depends on video bitrate. At 100Mbps (typical for 4K60), 256GB stores approximately 5.5 hours of video. At 60Mbps (4K30), approximately 9.5 hours. At higher bitrates like 400Mbps (professional 4K), about 1.4 hours. The V30 rating ensures the card can sustain 30MB/s (240Mbps) write speed required for most 4K recording.
faqs.decisionGuide: 256GB provides 5-10 hours of 4K video depending on quality settings.

faqs.question: What does A2 rating mean?
faqs.answer: A2 (Application Performance Class 2) rating guarantees minimum 4000 IOPS read and 2000 IOPS write performance for running apps directly from the microSD card. This is important for Android devices using Adoptable Storage or running apps from SD card. A2 cards provide significantly better app launch and execution performance compared to A1 or non-rated cards. For best app performance, choose A2 rated cards.
faqs.decisionGuide: A2 rating ensures good performance for apps installed on microSD.

faqs.question: Is this card compatible with Nintendo Switch?
faqs.answer: Yes, the FM256-V30 is fully compatible with Nintendo Switch and Switch Lite. The 256GB capacity can store approximately 30-60 games depending on size. The high read speed ensures fast game loading. The card meets Nintendo's performance requirements for game storage and downloads. For Switch OLED or original Switch, this card provides excellent expansion capacity.
faqs.decisionGuide: Fully compatible with Nintendo Switch for game storage.

faqs.question: Can I use this card for security cameras?
faqs.answer: Yes, the FM256-V30 can be used in security cameras that support microSD storage. The high endurance of 256GB capacity provides days of continuous recording depending on resolution and quality settings. For 24/7 continuous recording, consider high-endurance cards specifically designed for surveillance use. For motion-activated recording, this card provides excellent capacity and reliability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Suitable for security cameras, especially motion-activated recording.

faqs.question: What is the difference between U3 and V30 ratings?
faqs.answer: U3 (UHS Speed Class 3) and V30 (Video Speed Class 30) both guarantee 30MB/s minimum sustained write speed. U3 is the older rating system, while V30 is newer and specifically designed for video recording. V30 testing is more stringent for sustained performance required by video cameras. For video recording, V30 is preferred, while U3 is sufficient for general use. This card carries both ratings for maximum compatibility.
faqs.decisionGuide: Both U3 and V30 indicate 30MB/s write - V30 preferred for video.
